findings.description: Page layout is disrupted with duplicated header content and an incorrectly placed footer.
findings.evidence: In the HTML for page 1, the right-aligned header text block ('ROY COOPER...', etc.) is rendered twice. Additionally, the page footer ('State of North Carolina | Division of Marine Fisheries...') is inserted into the middle of the upper page content, appearing before the main 'PROCLAMATION' title.

findings.description: Page footer text interrupts a continuous sentence across a page break.
findings.evidence: Paragraph III.B continues from page 1 to page 2 in the PDF ('...from' ending page 1, 'April 1...' starting page 2). In the HTML, the page footer text is inserted directly between these words, breaking the logical reading flow of the sentence.

findings.description: Page footer text interrupts a URL spanning a page break.
findings.evidence: A URL in section IV.J spans from page 2 to page 3 in the PDF. The HTML inserts the page footer text directly between the two halves of the URL ('...title-50/part-' and '648/subpart-i').

findings.description: Significant content duplication at the end of the document.
findings.evidence: In the HTML rendering, the final portion of the document—starting from the partial URL '648/subpart-i' through sections K, L, M, the signature block, and the date—is rendered twice sequentially.

findings.description: Signature image is missing in the duplicated ending section.
findings.evidence: While the first instance of the signature block in the HTML successfully includes the signature image, the mistakenly duplicated second instance drops the image entirely, replacing it with a blank line ('__________________').
